title: Wedelia bussei
intro: Wedelia bussei, also known as Mexican daisy or Wedelia, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia bussei include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Wedelia bussei is often made into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, with the plant's leaves and flowers being the primary parts used for preparation.
taxonomy: Wedelia bussei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Wedelia, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. The genus Wedelia is part of the tribe Heliantheae, which comprises daisies and sunflowers. This classification indicates Wedelia bussei's close relationship to other flowering plants. Further, the plant is also known to be a member of the subfamily Asteroideae.
